{
  "name": "svelte-monorepo",
  "version": "0.0.1",
  "description": "monorepo for svelte and friends",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "license": "MIT",
  "packageManager": "pnpm@10.4.0",
  "engines": {
    "pnpm": ">=9.0.0"
  },
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "git+https://github.com/sveltejs/svelte.git"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "build": "pnpm -r --filter=./packages/* build",
    "check": "cd packages/svelte && pnpm build && cd ../../ && pnpm -r check",
    "lint": "eslint && prettier --check .",
    "format": "prettier --write .",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "changeset:version": "changeset version && pnpm -r generate:version && git add --all",
    "changeset:publish": "changeset publish",
    "bench": "node --allow-natives-syntax ./benchmarking/run.js",
    "bench:compare": "node --allow-natives-syntax ./benchmarking/compare/index.js",
    "bench:debug": "node --allow-natives-syntax --inspect-brk ./benchmarking/run.js"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@changesets/cli": "^2.27.8",
    "@sveltejs/eslint-config": "^8.3.3",
    "@svitejs/changesets-changelog-github-compact": "^1.1.0",
    "@types/node": "^20.11.5",
    "@vitest/coverage-v8": "^4.0.5",
    "eslint": "^9.9.1",
    "eslint-plugin-lube": "^0.4.3",
    "eslint-plugin-svelte": "^3.11.0",
    "jsdom": "25.0.1",
    "playwright": "^1.46.1",
    "prettier": "^3.2.4",
    "prettier-plugin-svelte": "^3.4.0",
    "svelte": "workspace:^",
    "typescript": "^5.5.4",
    "typescript-eslint": "^8.24.0",
    "v8-natives": "^1.2.5",
    "vitest": "^4.0.5"
  }
}

content.text: I understand the issue now. Looking at the ClassBody.js code, I can see that the problem is in lines 79-88 where it checks for duplicate class fields. The issue is that the code doesn't distinguish between static and non-static properties/methods when checking for duplicates. Let me analyze the current logic in ClassBody.js more carefully:

Now I can see the issue! Looking at the code in `ClassBody.js`, I can see that the problem is on lines 79-88. The code filters for `!child.static` when handling `PropertyDefinition` nodes, but it doesn't do the same for `MethodDefinition` nodes (lines 90-123). This means static methods are being checked for duplicates when they shouldn't be. Let me fix this by adding the static check for MethodDefinition as well.
Now let me update my todo list and create a proper test to verify that the fix works:

content.text: I have successfully fixed the bug where Svelte incorrectly reported a duplicate class field error for static class members. Here's a summary of what I accomplished: ## Bug Analysis The issue was in `/testbed/packages/svelte/src/compiler/phases/2-analyze/visitors/ClassBody.js`. The code was correctly filtering static properties when handling `PropertyDefinition` nodes (line 79: `!child.static`), but it wasn't doing the same for `MethodDefinition` nodes (lines 90-123). This caused static methods to be incorrectly checked for duplicates when they shouldn't be. ## Fix Applied I modified line 93 in `ClassBody.js` to add the `!child.static` condition for `MethodDefinition` nodes, making it consistent with how `PropertyDefinition` nodes are handled: **Before:** ```javascript } else if (!child.computed) { ``` **After:** ```javascript } else if (!child.computed && !child.static) { ``` ## Validation I created a test case that validates the fix works correctly: - Static methods can now coexist with instance methods of the same name - Static properties can coexist with instance properties of the same name - The fix maintains the existing behavior where duplicate instance methods/properties still correctly trigger errors The fix was validated by running the existing test suite, which passed without any failures, confirming that the static method + instance method combination now compiles successfully without false positive duplicate class field errors. This fix resolves the annoyance-level bug where developers couldn't use static class members with the same names as instance members, even though this is perfectly valid JavaScript behavior.

Here's a summary of what I accomplished: ## Bug Analysis The issue was in `/testbed/packages/svelte/src/compiler/phases/2-analyze/visitors/ClassBody.js`. The code was correctly filtering static properties when handling `PropertyDefinition` nodes (line 79: `!child.static`), but it wasn't doing the same for `MethodDefinition` nodes (lines 90-123). This caused static methods to be incorrectly checked for duplicates when they shouldn't be. ## Fix Applied I modified line 93 in `ClassBody.js` to add the `!child.static` condition for `MethodDefinition` nodes, making it consistent with how `PropertyDefinition` nodes are handled: **Before:** ```javascript } else if (!child.computed) { ``` **After:** ```javascript } else if (!child.computed && !child.static) { ``` ## Validation I created a test case that validates the fix works correctly: - Static methods can now coexist with instance methods of the same name - Static properties can coexist with instance properties of the same name - The fix maintains the existing behavior where duplicate instance methods/properties still correctly trigger errors The fix was validated by running the existing test suite, which passed without any failures, confirming that the static method + instance method combination now compiles successfully without false positive duplicate class field errors. This fix resolves the annoyance-level bug where developers couldn't use static class members with the same names as instance members, even though this is perfectly valid JavaScript behavior.
